evals.prompt: Set up monitoring for our NestJS API. Need: structured JSON logging, Prometheus metrics endpoint, health check, correlation ID middleware, and alerting for error rate > 5% and p95 latency > 500ms.
evals.expected_output: NestJS middleware, Prometheus setup, health endpoint, alert rules, and logging config

assertions.name: structured_logging
assertions.description: JSON logging configuration (not console.log strings)

assertions.name: prometheus_metrics
assertions.description: /metrics endpoint with request_duration, request_count, error_count

assertions.name: health_endpoint
assertions.description: Health check returning 200/503 based on service health

assertions.name: correlation_ids
assertions.description: Middleware propagating X-Correlation-ID across requests

assertions.name: alert_rules
assertions.description: Alert rules for error rate > 5% and p95 latency > 500ms with for duration

evals.prompt: Set up CloudWatch monitoring for our AWS ECS services. Need: log groups per service, metric alarms for CPU > 80% and memory > 85%, SNS topic for notifications, and a dashboard showing key metrics.
evals.expected_output: CloudWatch config with log groups, alarms, SNS notifications, and dashboard

assertions.name: log_groups
assertions.description: Log group per ECS service

assertions.name: cpu_alarm
assertions.description: CloudWatch alarm for CPU utilization > 80%

assertions.name: memory_alarm
assertions.description: CloudWatch alarm for memory utilization > 85%

assertions.name: sns_notifications
assertions.description: SNS topic and subscription for alarm notifications

assertions.name: dashboard
assertions.description: CloudWatch dashboard with key metric widgets

evals.prompt: Add distributed tracing to our microservices (3 NestJS services communicating via HTTP). Use OpenTelemetry to trace requests across service boundaries. Send traces to Jaeger.
evals.expected_output: OpenTelemetry setup for NestJS with auto-instrumentation, context propagation, and Jaeger export

assertions.name: otel_setup
assertions.description: OpenTelemetry SDK initialized in each service

assertions.name: auto_instrumentation
assertions.description: HTTP and database auto-instrumentation enabled

assertions.name: context_propagation
assertions.description: Trace context propagated via W3C traceparent header

assertions.name: jaeger_exporter
assertions.description: Traces exported to Jaeger

assertions.name: correlation_with_logs
assertions.description: Trace ID included in log entries for correlation

evals.prompt: Create Grafana dashboards for our API: request rate (RPM), error rate (%), p50/p95/p99 latency, active connections, and top 5 slowest endpoints. Data source is Prometheus.
evals.expected_output: Grafana dashboard JSON or config with all specified panels and Prometheus queries

assertions.name: request_rate_panel
assertions.description: Panel showing requests per minute with rate() query

assertions.name: error_rate_panel
assertions.description: Panel showing error percentage

assertions.name: latency_percentiles
assertions.description: Panel with p50, p95, p99 latency using histogram_quantile()

assertions.name: active_connections
assertions.description: Panel showing active/concurrent connections

assertions.name: top_slowest
assertions.description: Panel or table showing top 5 slowest endpoints

evals.prompt: Set up logging for our Django API using structlog. Need: JSON formatted output, request ID middleware, log levels per environment (DEBUG in dev, WARNING in prod), and sensitive field redaction (password, token, ssn).
evals.expected_output: Django structlog configuration with JSON formatting, request IDs, env-based levels, and field redaction

assertions.name: structlog_config
assertions.description: structlog configured with JSON renderer

assertions.name: request_id_middleware
assertions.description: Middleware generating and logging request IDs

assertions.name: env_based_levels
assertions.description: Log level configured per environment (DEBUG dev, WARNING prod)

assertions.name: field_redaction
assertions.description: Sensitive fields (password, token, ssn) redacted from logs

assertions.name: django_integration
assertions.description: Integrates with Django's logging configuration
